title: EFASL Appoints New Committee for 2025/26
text: The Eisenhower Fellowships Association of Sri Lanka has appointed its governing committee for the 2025/26 term. Mr. Heminda Jayaweera (EF 2021) has been appointed as Chairman, with Ms. Asiri Hewamalage (EF 2024) as Secretary and Mr. Dhananath Fernando (EF 2022) as Treasurer. The committee also includes Mr. Chevaan Daniel (EF 2017), Ms. Seroshi Nandasiri (EF 2024), and Ms. Nevindaree Premarathne (EF 2025) as committee members. The association looks forward to a productive term under this new leadership.

title: New Interim Committee Appointed at EFASL AGM 2017
text: At the Annual General Meeting held on December 07, 2017, a new Interim Committee was appointed to steer the Eisenhower Fellowships Association of Sri Lanka. Mr. J.C. Weliamuna (EF 2004 SARP) was appointed as Chairman, with Mr. Ruwanthie de Chikera (EF 2014 MNP) as Secretary and Mr. Jayantha Fernando (EF 2012 SARP) continuing as Treasurer. Mr. Harsha Purasinghe (EF 2014 MNP) was appointed to the newly created role of Secretary Innovation, reflecting the association's growing focus on technology and innovation. The committee also welcomes Mr. Dayananda Gunawardena (EF 1986 MNP) and Mr. Nayana Mawilmada (EF 2017 MESA) as committee members. The association looks forward to a productive term under this new leadership.

title: ESASL AGM 2013 Elects New Committee for 2013/14
text: The Annual General Meeting held on November 30, 2013 at Colombo elected a new governing committee for the 2013/14 term. Dr. Paikiasothy Saravanamuttu (2004 SRP) was elected as Chairman, with Mr. Saliya Peiries (2012 SRP) as Secretary and Mr. Jayantha Fernando (2012 SRP) continuing as Treasurer. The committee also includes Mr. Chrishantha Weliamuna, Mrs. Susie Perera, and Mrs. Kanchana Weerakoon as committee members. The outgoing leadership of Mr. Chandra Jayaratne (Past Chairman) and Mr. Dayananda Gunawardena (Past Secretary and Treasurer) were warmly recognised for their contributions to the association.

title: EFASL Establishes Governing Committee for 2008/09
text: The Eisenhower Fellowships Association of Sri Lanka appointed its governing committee for the 2008/09 term. Mr. Chandra Jayaratne (EF 1989 MNP) was appointed Chairman, and Mr. H. Dayananda Gunawardena (EF 1986 MNP) took on the combined role of Secretary and Treasurer. Committee members included Mr. Chrishantha Weliamuna (2004 SRP), Mrs. Kala Peiris De Costa (2004 SRP), and Mrs. Subashini Rasanja (2007 MNP). The committee continued to build on the association's foundation of fostering leadership, international networking, and knowledge sharing among Eisenhower Fellows in Sri Lanka.

title: Tech Entrepreneur Drives Social Impact
text: As a young technology-focused entrepreneur, Harsha Purasinghe (Sri Lanka 2014) was instrumental in initiating and leading a unique social innovation in the aftermath of an Indian Ocean tsunami in 2004.

title: On the Road with George and Carrie
text: EF President George de Lama and his wife Carrie Holt de Lama have been on the move over the last few weeks, visiting eight countries in February and March to meet with Fellows, Trustees, donors and other friends of EF from Taipei to Istanbul.

title: Sri Lanka’s biggest online retailer takes more slices of the “On-Demand” Economy
EF Fellow - Jayantha Fernando honored with LifeTime Award
Humanitarian Relief in Sri Lanka
Eran picked for finance, Harsha for economic affairs
The eisenhower day celebrations conference 2016
Stephanie's visit to Colombo
text: Ms. Stephanie Veit (EF Executive Director- Advancement and External Relations) visited Sri Lanka in June this year. The visit kicked off with meetings at the Sri Lankan parliament, where she had discussions with Hon. Harsha de Silva (Deputy Minister of Policy Planning and Economic Development, EF MNP 2011) as well as Hon. Eran Wickramaratne (Deputy Minister of Highways, Education and Investment Promotion, EF SRP 2004). Stephanie also spent time at the US Embassy, where she met Senior Officers to discuss how to expand the EF program in the region. She was hosted by Nathan Sivagananathan (EF MNP 2015) for an evening of fellowship with the Eisenhower Alumni network in Sri Lanka, which was also attended by Mr. Zeeshan Usmani (EF 2012) and his team from Predictifyme. Stephanie’s visit concluded with a visit to the historical town of Galle, where she spent the day enjoying some sun, sand and cultural heritage before returning to the United States. It was a pleasure to have Stephanie in Sri Lanka and we look forward to seeing her here again soon.

title: BRIGITTE DANIEL - 2011 USA EISENHOWER FELLOW
2002 Berlin Germany Eisenhower Fellowships Conference
Youth Leadership Program 2026 – Second Cohort Launched
text:
We are proud to announce the launch of the second cohort of the Youth Leadership Program on the 19th of May 2026 at TRACE Expert City, Colombo. This year, we have selected 15 exceptional young leaders representing diverse backgrounds, regions, and communities across Sri Lanka, all united by a shared commitment to driving positive change and creating meaningful impact. These young changemakers embody the spirit of leadership, innovation, inclusion, and service that our nation needs for the future.

As we begin this journey together, we are inspired by the passion, resilience, and vision of this cohort, who are already championing transformation within their communities and beyond.

We extend our sincere gratitude to Eisenhower Fellowships for their continued support and belief in empowering the next generation of leaders, and to all Sri Lankan Eisenhower Fellows for their unwavering commitment, mentorship, and support in making this initiative possible.

Together, we continue to invest in leadership that connects, inspires, and transforms Sri Lanka for generations to come.

title: Inaugural Youth Leadership Program 2025 Concludes Successfully
text:
We successfully concluded the inaugural Youth Leadership Program 2025 on Friday, 17th October, at Trace Expert City, Bay 12, Colombo. Eight exceptional young leaders completed this transformative journey, marking the beginning of a new generation of purpose-driven changemakers.

We were deeply honored to have Senior Eisenhower Fellows join the occasion, sharing their wisdom and inspiration. Dulith Herath and Bhavani Fonseka delivered powerful reflections on their Eisenhower Fellowship experiences, leaving the youth leaders with invaluable lessons on leadership, courage, and impact.

This milestone reaffirms our commitment to nurturing the next generation of resilient, ethical, and visionary leaders for Sri Lanka and beyond.

title: Eisenhower Fellowships Sri Lanka Fellows Meet up
Eisenhower Fellowships Sri Lanka - March Dinner Meet up
text: This was organised to recognise 2 eisenhower fellows who became Deputy Ministers, Sharing EF Journeys of Ruwanthi De Chickera and Harsha Purasinghe followed by recognising our latest EF MNP fellow Nathan Sivagananathan.

title: YLP Dialogue with Dr. Paikiasothy Saravanamuttu (EF Sri Lanka)
text:
Last week EFSL Youth Leadership Program featured an engaging dialogue with Dr. Paikiasothy Saravanamuttu, sharing his wisdom and perspectives with the next generation of leaders. Our Youth Fellows had the opportunity to learn from his vast experience and insights on democracy, governance, citizenship, and Sri Lanka's future.

#EFSL #EisenhowerFellowships #YouthLeadership #Leadership #SriLanka #Efjourney

title: YLP Cohorts Engage in Dialogue with His Excellency Santosh Jha at India House
text:
An inspiring evening at India House, Colombo, as Youth Leadership Fellows from Cohort 1 and Cohort 2 of the Youth Leadership Program, organized by the Eisenhower Fellowship Sri Lanka Association, had the privilege of engaging in a thought-provoking dialogue with His Excellency Santosh Jha.

The discussion covered a diverse range of topics, including politics, diplomacy, regional security, artificial intelligence, culture, youth leadership, innovation, and people-to-people connections. It was a truly enriching exchange that broadened perspectives, sparked curiosity, and encouraged meaningful reflection among our young leaders.

We extend our sincere gratitude to His Excellency and the team at the High Commission of India for their warm hospitality and for creating this valuable opportunity for learning and engagement. Conversations such as these play an important role in shaping the next generation of leaders while strengthening the enduring ties between Sri Lanka and India.

title: Inspiring YLP Session with Senior Eisenhower Fellow Eran Wickramaratne
text:
Our Youth Fellows had another inspiring session with Senior Eisenhower Fellow Eran Wickramaratne, who shared his remarkable life journey, transformative EF experience, and invaluable leadership lessons. His wisdom left them deeply inspired and better prepared to lead with purpose.

#efjourney #youthleadership

title: YLP Dialogue with Visionary Entrepreneur Harsha Purasinghe
text:
As part of the Eisenhower Fellowships Sri Lanka Youth Leadership Program, our fellows had the privilege of engaging with visionary entrepreneur and global Sri Lankan, Harsha Purasinghe.

Harsha’s journey — marked by innovation, resilience, and leadership — offered invaluable lessons and inspiration to our youth fellows.

We extend our heartfelt thanks to Harsha for generously sharing his time, insights, and experiences to help shape the next generation of Sri Lankan leaders.

#efjourney #youthleadership

title: YLP Session with Nathan Sivagananathan at Atman Group
text:
Today, our Youth Fellows had the privilege of an inspiring session with senior Eisenhower Fellow, Nathan Sivagananathan, at his office at Atman Group. Nathan, a visionary entrepreneur and investor, shared his remarkable journey of building businesses, driving innovation, and creating opportunities for others. His candid insights on entrepreneurship, leadership, and resilience offered our fellows invaluable lessons they can carry forward in their own journeys.

#efyouthfellowshipsrilanka #efjourney

title: YLP Discussion with Nayana Mavilmada (President – Properties at John Keells)
text:
The Youth Fellows had an engaging and insightful discussion today with Nayana Mavilmada, EF Fellow 2017 and President – Properties at John Keells Holdings. The conversation spanned a wide range of topics—from urban planning and social norms to equitable and inclusive development and the role of technology in shaping the future.

A truly inspiring session—thank you, Nayana, for sharing your time, experience, and valuable perspectives with us!

title: YLP Session featuring Geetha Tharmaratnam
text:
Another successful session of the Youth Leadership Program was held at Trace Sri Lanka, featuring Geetha Tharmaratnam, Eisenhower Fellow and Chief Impact Investment Officer of the World Health Organization Foundation. The session focused on global impact investments, scientific research in health, and the evolving roles of genetics and AI.

#youthleadershipprogram #efjourney

title: YLP Cohort Meets World-Renowned Astronomer Prof. Chandra Wickramasinghe
text:
Yesterday, our Eisenhower Fellowships Sri Lanka-Youth Fellows had the privilege of meeting Prof. Chandra Wickramasinghe, the world-renowned astronomer and astrobiologist.

In an engaging conversation, we explored science, life, and leadership, gaining wisdom from his decades of groundbreaking research and fearless pursuit of knowledge.

A truly inspiring exchange that reminded us of the power of curiosity, bold ideas, and dialogue across generations.

#EisenhowerFellowships #YouthLeadership #Inspiration #SriLanka #efjourney

title: YLP Session with Ligwina Hananto (EF Indonesia)
text: The Youth Leadership Program hosted a virtual session with Ligwina Hananto, Founder and CEO of QM Financial and an Eisenhower Fellow from Indonesia. She shared valuable insights on leadership, entrepreneurship, and financial literacy while engaging with participants through an interactive discussion and Q&A session.

title: YLP Session with Shinto Nugroho (EF Singapore)
text: The Youth Leadership Program conducted a virtual session with Shinto Nugroho, providing participants with insights into leadership, personal growth, and his professional journey. The interactive discussion encouraged youth leaders to reflect on their own aspirations while engaging directly with an accomplished global leader.

title: YLP Discussion with Former President Her Excellency Chandrika Bandaranaike Kumaratunga
text: The Youth Leadership Program hosted a special discussion with former President Chandrika Bandaranaike Kumaratunga. Fellows engaged in meaningful conversations on national unity, governance, women's leadership, and policymaking, gaining valuable perspectives from her extensive public service experience.

title: YLP Session with Eran Wickramaratne (EF Sri Lanka)
text: Senior Eisenhower Fellow Eran Wickramaratne joined the Youth Leadership Program for an engaging session on economic policy, governance, and leadership. The discussion enabled participants to explore contemporary national issues while learning from his experiences in public service and finance.

title: YLP Engagement with Erin Hillman and Katherine Leventhal from EF Head Quarters
text: Youth Leadership Program participants engaged in an interactive session with Erin Hillman and Katherine Leventhal from Eisenhower Fellowships. The discussion provided an overview of the global fellowship program, its mission, leadership development opportunities, and future pathways for engagement.

title: YLP Discussion with Paul Maria (EF France)
text: The Youth Leadership Program welcomed Eisenhower Fellow Paul Maria from France for an engaging discussion with participants. He shared his leadership experiences and global perspectives while encouraging meaningful dialogue with the youth leaders.

title: YLP Session with Veronica Pascual Boé (EF Spain) and Mothanna Gharaibeh (EF Jordan)
text: The Youth Leadership Program hosted an inspiring session with Eisenhower Fellows Verónica Pascual Boé of Spain and Mothanna Gharaibeh of Jordan. They shared their experiences in leadership, innovation, entrepreneurship, and public service while discussing the development of technology, investment, and STEM ecosystems with the youth leaders.
